evidence_quotes.quote: dark patterns and online manipulation to purchase or to use certain services
dark patterns and manufactured scarcity found on a lot of digital storefronts
addictive gatcha or gambling mechanics seen in most modern phone games and live action games

Where the consumer is led to believe they purchased a product, while in reality they only purchased a licence that can be terminated with little notice leaving the consumer with no return for their monetary investment.

paragraphs.text: I'm just a healthcare worker with a lot of online hobbies. I really like the awareness being drawn and regulations being made for online fairness, I have personally seen countless people being abused by dark patterns and online manipulation to purchase or to use certain services, I have even fallen victim to them myself. The digital world has created a new world of options for trade and international commerce, but we as consumers are playing catch-up with regards to rights and protections. Nothing incapsulates this better then the modern use of dark patterns and addictive gatcha or gambling mechanics seen in most modern phone games and live action games. A concern I would also love to see addressed by lawmakers is the clarification of digital purchases, it is often unclear if you are purchasing a product as you would with a car or any physical product or if you are purchasing the rights to use a digital product similar to renting a car. Personally I don't thing the Digital Fairness Act is broad enough in the fields it covers and could benefit the consumers more by also addressing the modern abuse of licenses most commonly seen in live service video games such as the CREW. Where the consumer is led to believe they purchased a product, while in reality they only purchased a licence that can be terminated with little notice leaving the consumer with no return for their monetary investment. This in my eyes at least is similar to the abuse of dark patterns and manufactured scarcity found on a lot of digital storefronts. Thank you for reading and I whole heartedly support this act as a consumer.
